categories.description: Identifiers or paths that would leak private owner, device, source-root or account information in public artifacts.
Identifiers that are acceptable only because the repository is private and the artifact is operationally local.
categories.check_behavior: security-check may suppress only with explicit baseline entry

categories.description: Generated review examples that quote local roles or worker names and are not public export inputs.
categories.check_behavior: security-check may suppress only for exact generated paths

categories.description: Scripts that contain local context strings needed for detection, but not secrets.
categories.check_behavior: security-check may suppress only personal-pattern findings, never secrets

categories.description: API keys, tokens, private keys and credentials.

initial_rules.rationale: Source-root discovery and decision artifacts are local operational records.
OneDrive sync diagnostics can include local account/path hints.
Owner review packets are private governance artifacts.
Private library diagnostics are not public export inputs.
Generated Orbit review fixtures need separate public-export filtering.
These scripts intentionally detect local operational paths and names.

implementation_contract: Baseline must be explicit data, not hard-coded broad regex weakening.
Baseline can suppress only personal identifier findings, never secret patterns.
Every suppressed finding must retain count and category in the report.
Public export workflows must keep failing on must_redact and secret_never_allowed.
New paths are not auto-allowed; they require a baseline rule update.
next_actions: Patch scripts/security-check.mjs to load the baseline and report suppressed counts.
Run npm run security:check and verify secrets still hard-fail.
Only after that rerun npm run quality:check.
